steps.title: Identify the contradiction
steps.content: The key signal is the clash between observation and explanation: quasars are believed to have formed so early (observed fact) yet conditions are believed to have been ill-suited to their creation (theoretical obstacle). This contradiction is what prompts the “which suggests that.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
When observations contradict theory, the inference is that our understanding is incomplete.

Choice B directly expresses this: our scientific understanding of quasar formation or the early universe “may be incomplete”.

Choices A, C, and D either merely restate facts already given or invent comparisons not supported by the passage.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
